messages.content: love

Love is a complex and multifaceted emotion that encompasses a deep affection, attachment, or care for someone or something. It can be felt towards family members, friends, romantic partners, pets, or even hobbies and interests. Love often involves selflessness, trust, and a desire for the well-being and happiness of the person or thing being loved. It can manifest in various ways, including through acts of kindness, emotional support, or simply through the strong bond and connection that is formed. Love is a fundamental aspect of human existence and has the power to bring joy, fulfillment, and meaning to our lives.

The model of long-term leadership's main aim is to achieve ______
Select one:
a. Envisioning goals
b. Effective leadership
c. Empowerment
d. Establish values
b. Effective leadership

Negligence is defined as
Select one:
a. Failure to exercise the necessary degree of care for the safety of the learner
b. A teacher being in loco parentis at all given times
c. Making sure all learners are safe all the time
a. Failure to exercise the necessary degree of care for the safety of the learner

Define assertiveness
Select one:
a. Emotional behavior resulting in conflict
b. It is the best way to improve yourself
c. Participate in conflict situations
d. To be able to control your emotions, thoughts and behavior in an appropriate way
d. To be able to control your emotions, thoughts, and behavior in an appropriate way

The Constitution of South Africa is not considered as a ________
Select one:
a. Supreme law
b. No Policy or legislation may be in conflict with this law
c. Legislation
d. Policy document
c. Legislation

Actively engaging learners is a factor of
Select one:
a. Communicate goals to learners
b. Keeping the learning-teaching environment alive
c. Draw up a management plan
d. Your objectives as a teacher
b. Keeping the learning-teaching environment alive

Classroom rules should not ______
Select one:
a. Should be in line with the Code of Conduct
b. Affirmation of learners
c. Punishment must fit offense
d. Involve learners in the process.
c. Punishment must fit offense

Natural person is defined as
Select one:
a. Criminal justice
b. All humans
c. Juristic person of the law
d. Social justice
b. All humans

This group is technologically savvy
Select one:
a. School Governing Body
b. Millennial generation
c. Educators
d. Parents
b. Millennial generation

Which of the following is not the duty of the parent as stated in the South African School's Act of 1996b
Select one:
a. Liability for property damage
b. School Attendance of the Learner
c. Attending sport activities of your child
d. Paying School fees
c. Attending sport activities of your child

The appraisal system for education is known as
Select one:
a. IQMS
b. MCQ
c. QMSI
d. QMS
a. IQMS (Integrated Quality Management System)

Managing your objectives includes formulating your objectives using this strategy
Select one:
a. OBE
b. SMART
c. TIME
d. SWOT
b. SMART (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound)

What is educator leadership
Select one:
a. Management of communication
b. Empowerment of the educator-self
c. It is the art of influencing learners to achieve learning outcomes
d. Management of educators
c. It is the art of influencing learners to achieve learning outcomes

Which learning style does not fit the Millennial generation
Select one:
a. Experimental activities
b. Technology
c. Group work
d. Reading
d. Reading

The final judgement of the Pillay case ended in the _____
Select one:
a. Regional Magistrate's court
b. Constitutional court
c. Appeal Court
d. High Court
b. Constitutional court

Decoloniality theory originated from
Select one:
a. South African indigenous knowledge
b. The Bandung Conference of 1995
c. White population interests
d. European empires
d. European empires

What characterizes the fourth industrial revolution, according to Schwab?
Select one:
a. Mass production and division of labour
b. Introduction of electronics and IT
c. Fusion of physical, digital and biological worlds
d. Advent of electricity
c. Fusion of physical, digital and biological worlds

Discursive power explains
Select one:
a. Shaping social, political and cultural agendas
b. Access to resources and control
c. Administrative and legal authority
d. Contestations over meanings
a. Shaping social, political and cultural agendas

Inequality is defined as the state of
Select one:
a. Not being equal in status, rights and opportunities
b. Being in power relations
c. Being subject to institutional policies
d. Equal rights and opportunities
a. Not being equal in status, rights and opportunities

Power reaches into the lives of individuals through
Select one:
a. Learning processes
b. Inequality
c. Eurocentric epistemologies
d. Silent agreement
a. Learning processes

Institutional power relates to
Select one:
a. Contestations over meanings
b. Shaping social, political and cultural agendas
c. Strategies employed by groups and institutions in exercising authority
d. Access to resources and control over their allocation
c. Strategies employed by groups and institutions in exercising authority

ccording to Christie (2002), what continues to be a major predictor of social and schooling outcomes in South Africa?
Select one:
a. Poverty and unemployment
b. Racial segregation
c. Political independence
d. Educational reforms
b. Racial segregation

The resource differentials between schools in South Africa are a result of
Select one:
a. Post colonial theories
b. Apartheid and colonial legacies
c. Eurocentric epistemologies
d. Equal levels of provision
b. Apartheid and colonial legacies

The National Education Act No. 39 of 1867 in South Africa
Select one:
a. Focused on the decolonization of education
b. Closed mission schools
c. Enshrined Christian National Education
d. Established segregated and separate institutions
d. Established segregated and separate institutions

a. Equal expectations for all students
b. Compulsory schooling for all children
c. Differences in material provision, teacher qualifications and facilities
d. Adequate learning materials in all schools
c. Differences in material provision, teacher qualifications, and facilities

According to Levin (2018) what is the expected outcome of the fourth industrial revolution in terms of production?
Select one:
a. Shift from knowledge intensive to labour intensive production
b. Shift from production to services sector
c. Shift from labour intensive to knowledge and skills intensive production
d. No significant changes in production patterns
c. Shift from labour intensive to knowledge and skills intensive production

How would you define apartheid?
Select one:
a. The fusion of physical, digital and biological worlds
b. The shift from labour intensive to knowledge intensive production
c. Racial segregation and discrimination
d. The fourth industrial revolution
c. Racial segregation and discrimination

a. Colonialism defines culture, while coloniality defines labour
b. Colonialism refers to power relations, while coloniality refers to economic organization
c. Colonialism is political and economic, while coloniality extends beyond colonial administrations
d. Colonialism emerges from coloniality
c. Colonialism is political and economic, while coloniality extends beyond colonial administrations

Power relations are characterized by
Select one:
a. Power being absent in social relationships
b. Equal distribution of power
c. Power being subjected to functions
d. One sided dominance
d. One sided dominance

What changes occurred in education after 1994 in South Africa?
Select one:
a. Increased focus on vocational skills development
b. Shift towards knowledge-intensive production
c. Introduction of digital technologies in classrooms
d. Improved access to education for disadvantaged communities
d. Improved access to education for disadvantaged communities
